2606.09371.title: Capability-Aligned Hierarchical Learning for Tool-Augmented LLMs
2606.09371.paper_summary: Tool learning enables LLMs to invoke external tools to accomplish tasks. Prior studies have demonstrated the effectiveness of a hierarchical structure: a high-level policy handles global planning and decomposes tasks into manageable sub-tasks, and a low-level policy focuses on invoking tools to solve these sub-tasks. However, these works typically optimize the high-level and low-level policies separately, leading to planner-executor misalignment and limiting LLM performance on tool-use tasks. In this paper, we propose a method called Capability-Aligned Hierarchical Learning (CAHL), which leverages RLVR to jointly optimize both policies, enabling better alignment between the high-level planner and the low-level executor. Experiments on constrained tool-use benchmarks (API-Bank and BFCL) and an open-ended environment (Bamboogle) demonstrate the effectiveness of CAHL.
2606.09371.paper_summary_zh: 工具學習使大型語言模型（LLMs）能夠調用外部工具來完成任務。<lb/>先前的研究已經證明了分層結構的有效性：高層策略負責全局規劃並將任務分解為可管理的子任務，而低層策略則專注於調用工具來解決這些子任務。<lb/>然而，這些工作通常分別優化高層和低層策略，導致規劃者與執行者之間的不一致，並限制了LLM在工具使用任務上的表現。<lb/>在本文中，我們提出了一種稱為能力對齊分層學習（CAHL）的方法，該方法利用RLVR來聯合優化這兩種策略，從而實現高層規劃者與低層執行者之間的更好對齊。<lb/>在受限工具使用基準（API-Bank和BFCL）以及一個開放式環境（Bamboogle）上的實驗證明了CAHL的有效性。
2606.09371.author: Haotong Yang et.al.
2606.09371.authors: Haotong Yang, Ting Long, Yi Chang
